muxt generate
muxt check

# Without --use-receiver-type muxt does not look up an existing method, so the
# sse() method wrapping marshalJSON(sendX) has a synthesized signature: the
# wrapped send callback becomes func(any) error and the method returns nothing,
# matching the void SSE contract. The synthesized method appears on the
# RoutesReceiver interface.
grep 'Stream\(ctx context.Context, sendStatus func\(any\) error\)' template_routes.go

exec go test -race -count=1

-- template.gohtml --
{{- define "GET /events sse(Stream(ctx, marshalJSON(sendStatus)))" -}}{{- end -}}
-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.24
-- server.go --
package server

import (
	"context"
	"embed"
	"html/template"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templatesFS embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templatesFS, "*"))

type Server struct{}

type Status struct {
	OK bool `json:"ok"`
}

// Stream implements the synthesized interface method signature. muxt does not
// require the method to be declared with this signature for generation, but the
// concrete receiver must satisfy the synthesized RoutesReceiver interface.
func (Server) Stream(ctx context.Context, sendStatus func(any) error) {
	_ = sendStatus(Status{OK: true})
}
-- server_test.go --
package server

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"strings"
	"testing"
)

func TestStreamMarshalSendSynthesized(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	TemplateRoutes(mux, Server{})
	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/events", nil)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)
	if got := rec.Body.String(); !strings.Contains(got, `data: {"ok":true}`+"\n\n") {
		t.Fatalf("body = %q, want it to contain %q", got, `data: {"ok":true}`+"\n\n")
	}
}
